edges.description: Persistent complement dysregulation can produce local complement activation and complement-directed injury in affected adipose tissue.
Alternative pathway dysregulation also links APL/BSS to C3 glomerulopathy and membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis.
Ongoing alternative-pathway activation consumes complement C3, producing the low circulating C3 phenotype used in BSS/APL workup.
C3 nephritic factor positivity is the common complement-autoantibody readout of the alternative-pathway dysregulation branch in BSS/APL.
Low serum complement C3 is the biochemical readout of alternative complement pathway consumption in BSS/APL.
BSS/APL cohorts show autoimmune disease and complement autoantibody enrichment alongside complement dysregulation.
Elevated FD/adipsin can accelerate local alternative pathway activation in adipose tissue already exposed to C3NeF-associated dysregulation.
edges.source: Factor D/Adipsin Amplification in Adipose Tissue

edges.description: Elevated factor D/adipsin is a measurable marker of this adipose-linked alternative-complement amplification branch.
edges.source: Factor D/Adipsin Amplification in Adipose Tissue

edges.description: Complement-directed injury to adipocytes in affected subcutaneous adipose depots produces the clinical upper-body lipoatrophy phenotype.
Complement-directed adipocyte injury in affected upper-body depots manifests clinically as facial adipose-tissue loss.
The cephalocaudal lipoatrophy pattern includes loss of neck subcutaneous adipose tissue.
Affected upper-body subcutaneous adipose depots include the upper limbs.
edges.target: Loss of subcutaneous adipose tissue from upper limbs

edges.description: Affected upper-body subcutaneous adipose depots include the trunk.
edges.target: Loss of truncal subcutaneous adipose tissue

edges.description: Loss of subcutaneous adipose tissue reduces safe adipose lipid-storage capacity and creates the substrate for metabolic complications.
intermediate_mechanisms: complement-linked loss of upper-body subcutaneous adipose tissue

edges.description: Reduced adipose storage capacity predisposes to insulin resistance in lipodystrophy syndromes.
intermediate_mechanisms: reduced adipose lipid buffering after fat loss

edges.description: Reduced adipose storage capacity predisposes to elevated circulating triglycerides in lipodystrophy syndromes.
intermediate_mechanisms: reduced adipose lipid buffering after fat loss

edges.description: Reduced adipose storage capacity predisposes to ectopic hepatic lipid accumulation in lipodystrophy syndromes.
intermediate_mechanisms: reduced adipose lipid buffering after fat loss

edges.description: Circulating triglyceride concentration reports the hypertriglyceridemia branch of reduced adipose lipid-storage capacity.
intermediate_mechanisms: hypertriglyceridemia secondary to reduced adipose lipid storage

edges.description: Impaired adipogenesis and adipocyte metabolic gene expression may weaken adipocyte maintenance in affected depots.

edges.description: Alternative-pathway glomerular disease in BSS/APL can present as C3 glomerulopathy within the membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis spectrum.
Complement-mediated glomerular involvement can present with proteinuria.

edges.description: Higher blood triglyceride tracks the hypertriglyceridemia branch of reduced adipose lipid-storage capacity.

nodes.description: Active affected adipose tissue shows atrophied and dead adipocytes, abnormal extracellular matrix production, macrophage infiltration, and local IgG/C3/C5a/C5b-9 deposition, supporting direct complement-mediated tissue injury as a mechanism for progressive lipoatrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Evidence of ongoing complement activation on adipose tissue from an 11-year-old girl with Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Adipose tissue from the patient showed atrophied and dead adipocytes, an abnormal production of extracellular matrix, and a remarkable accumulation of infiltrating CD68 macrophages and adipocyte precursors (marked by c-Kit positiveness).

evidence.reference_title: Evidence of ongoing complement activation on adipose tissue from an 11-year-old girl with Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Simultaneous detection of IgG, C3, C5a and C5b-9 proved the ongoing complement activity and complement-directed injury within the adipose tissue.

evidence.reference_title: Evidence of ongoing complement activation on adipose tissue from an 11-year-old girl with Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Our results showed the first evidence that the complement system hyperactivation occurs within the adipose tissue and is linked with fat loss in patients with BSS.

nodes.description: Acquired partial lipodystrophy is strongly associated with alternative complement pathway dysregulation. C3 nephritic factor and other complement-directed autoantibodies can stabilize complement activation, leading to C3 consumption, low serum C3, and persistent complement activation.

evidence.reference_title: Immunological features of patients affected by Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: C3 hypocomplementemia and the presence of C3 nephritic factor (C3NeF), an autoantibody causing complement system over-activation, are common features among most patients affected by Barraquer-Simons syndrome (BSS), an acquired form of partial lipodystrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Immunological features of patients affected by Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Moreover, the finding of autoantibodies targeting complement system proteins points to complement dysregulation as a central pathological event in the development of BSS.

nodes.description: Autoimmune diseases and complement-directed autoantibodies are common in studied BSS cohorts.

evidence.reference_title: Immunological features of patients affected by Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Clinical data showed high prevalence of autoimmune diseases (38.5%), the majority of patients (61.5%) being positive for at least one of the autoantibodies tested.

nodes.description: Autologous fat grafting/lipotransfer can be used for soft-tissue restoration in disfiguring facial or upper-body lipoatrophy and has published case-report support for quality-of-life benefit.

evidence.reference_title: Barraquer-Simons syndrome: a unique patient's perspective on diagnosis, disease progression and recontouring treatment.
evidence.snippet: Treatment of lipoatrophy in these patients is limited to cosmetic restoration, and autologous fat grafting has shown sustained positive effects with no or very little loss of volume at follow-ups.

evidence.reference_title: Barraquer-Simons syndrome: a unique patient's perspective on diagnosis, disease progression and recontouring treatment.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, the treatment has resulted in considerable improvements in her quality of life and daily functioning.

evidence.reference_title: Lipotransfer provides effective soft tissue replacement for acquired partial lipodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: Autologous fat transfer is an established plastic and reconstructive procedure that is safe and minimally invasive and can be used to reconstruct a variety of soft tissue defects and has shown to be an effective treatment modality in patients with APL.

evidence.reference_title: Lipodystrophy Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: The metabolic abnormalities associated with lipodystrophy include insulin resistance, hypertriglyceridemia, and hepatic steatosis.

readouts.interpretation: Higher blood triglyceride tracks the hypertriglyceridemia branch of reduced adipose lipid-storage capacity.

evidence.reference_title: Complement Factor D (adipsin) Levels Are Elevated in Acquired Partial Lipodystrophy (Barraquer-Simons syndrome).
evidence.snippet: Laboratory findings such as low complement C3 and the presence of C3NeF were used during the diagnosis of BSS.

readouts.interpretation: Presence of C3 nephritic factor supports C3 convertase-stabilizing alternative pathway dysregulation.

nodes.description: C3 nephritic factor is the most frequently detected complement autoantibody in the studied BSS cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Immunological features of patients affected by Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: C3NeF was the most frequently found autoantibody (69.2% of cases), followed by anti-C3 (38.5%), and anti-P and anti-FB (30.8% each).

evidence.reference_title: Complement Factor D (adipsin) Levels Are Elevated in Acquired Partial Lipodystrophy (Barraquer-Simons syndrome).
evidence.snippet: We found significantly elevated FD levels in BSS cases compared with the remaining cohorts (p < 0.001).

readouts.interpretation: Elevated factor D/adipsin reports the adipose-linked alternative-complement amplification branch.
readouts.target: Factor D/Adipsin Amplification in Adipose Tissue

nodes.description: The same alternative pathway abnormalities implicated in APL/BSS overlap mechanistically with C3 glomerulopathy and membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis, where C3NeF stabilizes C3 convertase and can produce proteinuria, hematuria, hypertension, or kidney failure.

evidence.reference_title: Complement Factor D (adipsin) Levels Are Elevated in Acquired Partial Lipodystrophy (Barraquer-Simons syndrome).
evidence.snippet: The mechanistic connection with complement activation is further reinforced by the fact that 20% of BSS patients eventually develop C3G [1].

evidence.reference_title: C3 glomerulopathies: dense deposit disease and C3 glomerulonephritis.
evidence.snippet: The underlying cause of both DDD and C3Gn is an abnormal activation of the alternative complement pathway, which can result from acquired or genetic alteration.

evidence.reference_title: C3 glomerulopathies: dense deposit disease and C3 glomerulonephritis.
evidence.snippet: In acquired forms of DDD and C3GN, the dysregulation of the alternative pathway is commonly induced by the presence of C3 nephritic factors (C3NeFs), which are autoantibodies that stabilize C3 convertase.

nodes.description: Neck adipose-tissue loss is part of the classic upper-body distribution.

evidence.reference_title: Barraquer-Simons syndrome: a unique patient's perspective on diagnosis, disease progression and recontouring treatment.
evidence.snippet: This case report describes a female patient diagnosed with Barraquer-Simons syndrome, a rare form of acquired partial lipodystrophy characterised by symmetrical loss of adipose tissue from face, neck, upper extremities and the trunk with onset in early childhood.

nodes.description: Low circulating C3 is a frequent biochemical and immunologic hallmark, often occurring with C3 nephritic factor.

evidence.reference_title: Immunological features of patients affected by Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: C3, C4 and FB levels were significantly reduced in patients with BSS as compared with healthy subjects.

nodes.description: Complement factor D, also called adipsin, is a key alternative-pathway protease largely produced by adipocytes and macrophages in adipose tissue. Elevated factor D in BSS may amplify local complement activation in the adipose-tissue environment and has been proposed as a diagnostic biomarker.
nodes.id: Factor D/Adipsin Amplification in Adipose Tissue

evidence.reference_title: Complement Factor D (adipsin) Levels Are Elevated in Acquired Partial Lipodystrophy (Barraquer-Simons syndrome).
evidence.snippet: Complement Factor D (FD) is a serine protease with a crucial role in the activation of the alternative pathway of the complement system, which is mainly synthesized by adipose tissue.

evidence.reference_title: Complement Factor D (adipsin) Levels Are Elevated in Acquired Partial Lipodystrophy (Barraquer-Simons syndrome).
evidence.snippet: We found significantly elevated FD levels in BSS cases compared with the remaining cohorts (p < 0.001).

evidence.reference_title: Complement Factor D (adipsin) Levels Are Elevated in Acquired Partial Lipodystrophy (Barraquer-Simons syndrome).
evidence.snippet: Our results suggest that FD could be a reliable diagnostic biomarker involved in the pathophysiology of BSS by promoting unrestrained local complement system activation in the adipose tissue environment.

nodes.description: Hepatic steatosis can occur as part of the metabolic complication spectrum of lipodystrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Lipodystrophy Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: The metabolic abnormalities associated with lipodystrophy include insulin resistance, hypertriglyceridemia, and hepatic steatosis.

nodes.description: Hyaluronic acid filler can be used for midface volume restoration in selected patients with BSS/APL.
nodes.id: Hyaluronic acid filler for facial volume loss

evidence.reference_title: Hyaluronic acid fillers for correcting midface volume deficit in Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Fillers, autologous fat transplant or cosmetic surgeries can be used to correct the volume deficit.

evidence.reference_title: Hyaluronic acid fillers for correcting midface volume deficit in Barraquer-Simons syndrome.
evidence.snippet: CONCLUSION: Hyaluronic acid filler is safe and effective for the management of volume loss in BSS.

nodes.description: Metabolic complications are variable in acquired partial lipodystrophy but include hypertriglyceridemia and other insulin-resistance complications when adipose storage capacity is reduced.

evidence.reference_title: Lipodystrophy Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: The metabolic abnormalities associated with lipodystrophy include insulin resistance, hypertriglyceridemia, and hepatic steatosis.

nodes.description: A human adipose biopsy case report found reduced expression of adipogenesis and mitochondrial-function genes in affected subcutaneous adipose tissue, suggesting impaired adipocyte maintenance may accompany complement-driven tissue loss.
nodes.id: Impaired Adipogenesis and Mitochondrial Gene Expression

evidence.reference_title: Impaired expression of mitochondrial and adipogenic genes in adipose tissue from a patient with acquired partial lipodystrophy (Barraquer-Simons syndrome): a case report.
evidence.snippet: The expression of marker genes of adipogenesis and adipocyte metabolism, including the master regulator PPARgamma, was down-regulated in subcutaneous adipose tissue from this patient.

evidence.reference_title: Impaired expression of mitochondrial and adipogenic genes in adipose tissue from a patient with acquired partial lipodystrophy (Barraquer-Simons syndrome): a case report.
evidence.snippet: Expression of genes related to mitochondrial function was reduced despite unaltered levels of mitochondrial DNA.

nodes.description: Insulin resistance is part of the variable metabolic complication spectrum of lipodystrophy, including acquired partial lipodystrophy when adipose storage capacity is reduced.

evidence.reference_title: Lipodystrophy Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Lipodystrophies are heterogeneous disorders characterized by varying degrees of body fat loss and predisposition to insulin resistance and its metabolic complications.

nodes.description: Facial fat loss is typically part of the cephalocaudal fat-loss pattern and often drives cosmetic and psychosocial morbidity.

evidence.reference_title: Barraquer-Simons syndrome: a unique patient's perspective on diagnosis, disease progression and recontouring treatment.
evidence.snippet: This case report describes a female patient diagnosed with Barraquer-Simons syndrome, a rare form of acquired partial lipodystrophy characterised by symmetrical loss of adipose tissue from face, neck, upper extremities and the trunk with onset in early childhood.

nodes.description: Upper-limb lipoatrophy follows the face and neck in the cephalocaudal pattern.

evidence.snippet: The authors report a case of a young woman, who presented with gradual loss of subcutaneous fat from the face, upper limbs, and trunk.

evidence.snippet: The authors report a case of a young woman, who presented with gradual loss of subcutaneous fat from the face, upper limbs, and trunk.

evidence.snippet: CONCLUSIONS: Our results confirmed the association between BSS, autoimmunity and C3 hypocomplementemia.

nodes.description: Complement-mediated glomerular disease is a clinically important complication of BSS/APL and may present as C3 glomerulopathy or membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis.

evidence.reference_title: C3 glomerulopathies: dense deposit disease and C3 glomerulonephritis.
evidence.snippet: Dense deposit disease (DDD) and C3 glomerulonephritis (C3GN) are types of membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis classified as C3 glomerulopathies.

evidence.reference_title: Complement Factor D (adipsin) Levels Are Elevated in Acquired Partial Lipodystrophy (Barraquer-Simons syndrome).
evidence.snippet: The mechanistic connection with complement activation is further reinforced by the fact that 20% of BSS patients eventually develop C3G [1].

nodes.description: Treatment of APL includes prevention and management of metabolic complications such as insulin resistance, hypertriglyceridemia, and hepatic steatosis when present.

evidence.reference_title: Lipodystrophy Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Management focuses on preventing and treating metabolic complications.

nodes.description: Metreleptin is a recombinant leptin analogue with expanded-access evidence in partial lipodystrophy patients with diabetes and/or hypertriglyceridemia. Applicability to classic APL/BSS is limited because the Ajluni cohort was a mixed partial-lipodystrophy cohort rather than APL-specific, and the abstract states FDA approval for generalized forms of lipodystrophy.
nodes.id: Metreleptin pharmacotherapy for severe metabolic complications

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and Safety of Metreleptin in Patients with Partial Lipodystrophy: Lessons from an Expanded Access Program.
evidence.snippet: Metreleptin is approved by the United States Food and Drug Administration for treatment of generalized forms of lipodystrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and Safety of Metreleptin in Patients with Partial Lipodystrophy: Lessons from an Expanded Access Program.
evidence.snippet: Study FHA101 (ClinicalTrials.gov identifier: NCT00677313) was an open-label, expanded-access, long-term clinical effectiveness and safety study in 23 patients with partial lipodystrophy and diabetes and/or hypertriglyceridemia with no prespecified leptin level.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and Safety of Metreleptin in Patients with Partial Lipodystrophy: Lessons from an Expanded Access Program.
evidence.snippet: CONCLUSIONS: Our clinical observations document the large heterogeneity and disease burden of partial lipodystrophy syndromes and suggest that metreleptin treatment benefits may extend to patients with partial lipodystrophy.

nodes.description: Evaluation by plastic and reconstructive surgery is appropriate when progressive lipoatrophy produces soft-tissue defects or psychosocial burden requiring reconstructive planning.

evidence.reference_title: Lipotransfer provides effective soft tissue replacement for acquired partial lipodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: Treatment is aimed at surgical correction of soft tissue destruction.

nodes.description: The defining phenotype is progressive lipoatrophy of upper-body subcutaneous adipose depots with sparing of lower extremities.

evidence.reference_title: Lipotransfer provides effective soft tissue replacement for acquired partial lipodystrophy.
evidence.snippet: It is a rare disease characterised by a gradual and progressive onset of lipoatrophy limited to the face, neck, upper limbs, thorax and abdomen and sparing the lower extremities.

nodes.description: Proteinuria is a presenting feature of complement-mediated glomerular disease that should be monitored when BSS/APL patients develop renal involvement.

evidence.reference_title: C3 glomerulopathies: dense deposit disease and C3 glomerulonephritis.
evidence.snippet: The presenting features of these diseases are variable and may include proteinuria, hematuria, hypertension, or kidney failure.

nodes.description: Loss of subcutaneous adipose tissue reduces adipose lipid-storage capacity. In lipodystrophy syndromes this predisposes to insulin resistance, hypertriglyceridemia, and hepatic steatosis, although these complications are variable in classic acquired partial lipodystrophy.

evidence.snippet: Lipodystrophies are heterogeneous disorders characterized by varying degrees of body fat loss and predisposition to insulin resistance and its metabolic complications.
